texto: But every time I go to church with you, I feel like everyone's trying to trap me in a room and convert me.' Which, in truth, they are. 'I want you, and your family, to love me for who I am,' he shouts, 'To let me be who I am.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Nicole Hardy: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “But every time I go to church with you, I feel like everyone's trying to trap me in a room and co…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, conversion, individuality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de individuality.

frase_original: But every time I go to church with you, I feel like everyone's trying to trap me in a room and convert me.' Which, in truth, they are. 'I want you, and your family, to love me for who I am,' he shouts, 'To let me be who I am.
